frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Personally I feel women look much better when they keep it simple and natural. Nowadays too much makeup and filters make it hard to know how someone actually looks, and then the same people say appearance should not matter at all. At the same time, basic grooming is important also, otherwise it looks like no self-care.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it subjects women to a double bind: criticizing visible makeup as deceptive while still expecting them to maintain attractiveness through 'basic grooming.' It encodes the 'natural beauty' preference, deception framing, and appearance-policing surface manifestations of the makeup paradox.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Santosh. Age 42. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are commenting under your real name on a local Facebook group post about a 23 year old woman who was harassed by two men while returning home alone at 11pm from her night shift at a BPO. The post has 400 comments. Most are expressing outrage at the men. You are writing your comment explaining why you think the woman's own choices that night contributed to what happened to her and what women who work night shifts need to understand about the city they live in.
